objectives: Full security audit of openrouter_api v0.4.0
Check OWASP Top 10, auth, input validation, dependency vulns
constraints: Library code only, no deployment/infra review
important_facts: bytes 1.10.1 has RUSTSEC-2026-0007 (integer overflow in BytesMut::reserve)
SecureApiKey has proper zeroize/no-clone/no-serialize but is accessible via pub fields on OpenRouterClient
No HTTPS enforcement on base_url or MCP server_url
MCP client error path leaks raw server response without redaction
elide() and redact_sensitive_content() can panic on multi-byte UTF-8
risks_issues: SEC-002 HIGH: pub fields expose SecureApiKey
SEC-004 HIGH: MCP error leaks raw response
SEC-005/006 MEDIUM: UTF-8 panic in elide/redact
SEC-007/008 MEDIUM: stderr logging in production
SEC-009 MEDIUM: no retry config validation

iteration.summary: Completed comprehensive security audit of openrouter_api v0.4.0 on feat/api-gap-phase1 branch. Found 15 security findings across all severity levels. The codebase shows good security awareness (SecureApiKey with zeroize, content redaction, streaming safety limits, MCP rate limiting) but has several actionable issues including a critical dependency vulnerability (bytes CVE), exposure of SecureApiKey through pub fields, missing HTTPS enforcement, and potential UTF-8 panics in string truncation. Priority remediation order provided.
next_actions: Fix SEC-002: Change pub to pub(crate) on client fields
Fix SEC-004: Add redaction to MCP error path
